segments.text: For over 100 years, Disney protected its characters like nuclear codes. Now, it's handed them to an AI company. Disney just invested $1 billion in open air.
And for the first time ever, fans can legally create AI videos using over 200 Disney, Marvel, Pixar, and Star Wars
characters on Sora, from Mickey Mouse to Darth Vader. But here's what the headlines missed. This isn't about money. It's about relevance. After years
of suing AI companies, Disney realized something critical. The creator economy is worth hundreds of billions of dollars and user generated content is watched
far more than studio content. Disney can't win by fighting that shift, so it's owning a piece of it. This isn't Disney abandoning movies. It's betting
on fans becoming creators, where 30-cond AI stories spread faster than any release. The advantage won't go to the biggest studio, but to the fastest
learner. Comment the character you try first and subscribe to full disclosure for what comes
